project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code

E.name: Proactive Intelligence
E.notes: Background sweep system built. Collection + Opus synthesis working. Haiku extraction from findings failing — needs debugging.

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Training wheels removed. Pre-tool security checks + output sanitization remain.

M.name: Background Sweeps
M.notes: 4 collectors (Slack, Gmail, Drive, Fireflies) built and tested. Parallel collection ~2 min. Opus CLI synthesis produces 27KB findings. Haiku extraction step failing — last blocker.

N.name: Context Planner
N.notes: Replaces preflight with Opus context planning. Built but not tested with real data (needs populated memory first). Has UX bug: no Slack progress feedback during planning.

infrastructure.local: macOS, DATABASE_URL=... npx tsx src/cli/index.ts start
infrastructure.dataVolume: /data (Claude CLI auth + gws config + debug workspaces)
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — deep path + sweep synthesis costs $0

currentSprint.name: Proactive Memory & Intelligent Context Assembly
goals: Drive collector (full paths, content snippets, staleness filter, code exclusion) ✅
Parallel collection (~2 min for all 4 sources) ✅
Synthesis mode (Write tool only, no MCP/Read overhead) ✅
Context planner (replaces preflight) ✅ (built, not fully tested)
Analysis mode (reduced tools when context is sufficient) ✅ (built, not tested)

currentSprint.progress: ~90% complete. Collection + synthesis pipeline working end-to-end. Last blocker: Haiku extraction from synthesis findings fails silently.

lastSession.type: Proactive Memory — sweep system, context planner, analysis mode, debugging
keyDecisions: Unified memory paradigm — removed people table, all knowledge as facts with entity tags
Workspace .md pipeline replaces findings JSON — model writes naturally, Haiku extracts
Removed 5 bespoke tools (list_people, list_commitments, slack_get_thread, etc.)
Background sweeps: single cadence for all sources, not per-source
Generic Collector interface for future extensibility (GitHub, QuickBooks, etc.)
Context planner replaces preflight — Opus gathers context + converses with user
Analysis mode: context planner decides if deep path needs research tools or not
Three routing modes only: FAST/MEDIUM/DEEP (removed DEEP_ANALYSIS — planner decides)
Sweep synthesis via --append-system-prompt-file (avoids E2BIG for large prompts)
Write tool for synthesis output (heredoc breaks on special chars in business data)
Slack cadence filter: skip channels with >21 day gaps between messages
Gmail noise filter: skip promotions, social, forums, calendar invites
Drive: exclude git repos, hidden files, code MIME types; staleness filter (6mo modified OR viewed)

immediateBacklog.title: Fix Haiku extraction from sweep findings — fails with empty error on 27KB chunked input
Context planner needs Slack progress feedback (goes silent during planning)
Drive collector still pulling code files (.ts as text/texmacs, .md, .py) — need better MIME exclusion or folder-based filtering

backlog.title: Make all tools available to all paths — routing decides usage, not tool restrictions
Artifact short-term memory — FIFO cache for read files/emails so follow-up questions don't re-fetch
Sweep status message in Slack ('don't bug me, I'm sweeping')
Ad hoc backfill CLI command with custom date ranges + higher limits
--session support for deep path thread continuity across Slack messages
Plugin adapter + CC memory bridge — shared brain via MCP
GitHub integration — code repos as a sweep source
Obsidian integration — .md export of memory for visibility

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md
Don't hardcode tunables in TypeScript — all operational values in config/default.json
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
Plan before coding — don't jump straight to implementation
Don't build bespoke tools/tables for specific entity types — unified memory handles it
Don't build point solutions — trust general-purpose tools (search_memory, etc.)
Don't hardcode whitelists/blacklists of MIME types or file patterns — use general filters
MCP stdio servers MUST redirect ALL logging to stderr
Deep path subprocess env: ALLOWLIST only, never pass Slack tokens
CLI sandbox blocks reads/writes outside project dir — workspace in cwd
CLI --append-system-prompt has arg size limit (~128KB) — use --append-system-prompt-file for large prompts
Heredoc (cat <<EOF) breaks on special chars in business data — use Write tool instead
Multiple .md file writes from deep path = multiple slow tool call turns — single file
Pre-flight must never imply starting without [PROCEED] sentinel
Brain reaction goes on user's message, not bot's confirmation
Long Slack messages MUST be chunked — msg_too_long silently drops content
Always notify user in Slack on errors — no silent failures
tsvector plainto_tsquery uses AND — use to_tsquery with | for OR
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS won't add new columns — ALTER TABLE for live migrations
NEVER run tests against production DATABASE_URL
GWS_CONFIG_B64 can silently disappear from Railway env vars — check entrypoint logs
Google Drive classifies .ts files as text/texmacs — can't rely on MIME type alone for filtering
